seoTitle: How to Fix Bank Link Errors on PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Troubleshoot PayPal bank linking errors by aligning your legal name, using manual entry, and completing micro-deposit verification.
intro: Errors like "We're sorry, we're not able to process your request" often come down to a mismatch between your PayPal profile and your bank's records. This guide covers the manual linking workaround and micro-deposit verification process to get past instant-link failures. It's for anyone stuck trying to add a checking or savings account.

steps.heading: Check your current linked instruments
steps.body: Go to Wallet and review what's currently linked before attempting to add a new bank account.

steps.heading: Trigger the manual linking form
steps.body: Click Link a bank account, and if instant linking fails, type your bank name incorrectly on purpose to force the manual setup form.

steps.heading: Enter your routing details carefully
steps.body: Select your account type and carefully input your correct routing and account number.

steps.heading: Agree to the micro-deposit method
steps.body: Click Agree and Link to fall back to the traditional 3-day micro-deposit verification process.

steps.heading: Confirm the micro-deposit amounts
steps.body: After about 48 hours, check your bank statement for two small deposits, then return to PayPal and enter those amounts to complete verification.

faq.question: Why does instant bank verification keep failing?
faq.answer: Instant verification can fail due to outdated routing information or a name mismatch, and the manual micro-deposit method often works around this.

faq.question: How long does micro-deposit verification take?
faq.answer: It typically takes about two to three business days for the small deposits to appear in your bank statement.

faq.question: Do I need to match my legal name exactly?
faq.answer: Yes, differences between your PayPal profile name and your bank's records are a common cause of persistent linking errors.

faq.question: What if I still see errors after micro-deposits?
faq.answer: Check the Resolution Center for any active identity challenges that might also be blocking the connection.
